name: ExtensionNutritionProduct_Characteristic
title: R5: Specifies descriptive properties of the nutrition product (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `NutritionProduct.characteristic` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`NutritionProduct.characteristic` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `NutritionProduct.characteristic` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `NutritionProduct.characteristic` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionProduct` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionProduct.characteristic` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:

Element `NutritionProduct.characteristic.type` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionProduct` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionProduct.characteristic.type` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Code specifying the type of characteristic
element.definition: A code specifying which characteristic of the product is being described (for example, colour, shape).

element.short: R5: The value of the characteristic (new)
element.definition: R5: `NutritionProduct.characteristic.value[x]` (new:Attachment, base64Binary, boolean, CodeableConcept, Quantity[SimpleQuantity], string)
element.comment:
Element `NutritionProduct.characteristic.value[x]` is part of an existing definition because parent element `NutritionProduct.characteristic` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `NutritionProduct.characteristic.value[x]` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionProduct` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionProduct.characteristic.value[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The description should be provided as a CodeableConcept, SimpleQuantity or an image.  The description can be a string only when these others are not available.
